ListItem.Para: Affected switch is deactivated open.
Associated audio control panel MIC/interphone switch operates normally

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the inoperative control wheel MIC/interphone switch, confirm it is deactivated open and verify the associated pilot’s audio control panel (ACP) MIC/interphone switch operates normally (AMM DDG 23-51-04)

ListItem.Para: Deactivate the inoperative control wheel MIC/interphone switch.
For captain’s switch inoperative, open circuit breaker FLT INTERPHONE-CAPT and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
For first officer’s switch inoperative, open circuit breaker FLT INTERPHONE-FO and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
Gain access to the inoperative MIC/interphone switch wiring harness assembly in the control wheel cavity beneath the approach chart plate.
Extract the three white wires SW3-1-24, SW3-2-24 and SW3-3-24 from the jiffy connectors.
Cap and stow the wires.
Reinstall the control wheel cover assembly and approach chart plate.
For captain’s switch inoperative, close FLT INTERPHONE-CAPT circuit breaker.
For first officer’s switch inoperative, close FLT INTERPHONE-FO circuit breaker.
Confirm the inoperative control wheel MIC/interphone switch is deactivated open and verify the associated pilot’s ACP MIC/interphone switch operates normally.
Push the FLT transmitter select switch on the captain’s and first officer’s ACPs and ensure the FLT MIC light is illuminated on both panels.
Ensure the FLT receiver light is illuminated and receiver volume control is properly adjusted on both ACPs.

Speak into the pilot’s microphone associated with the inoperative switch while simultaneously pushing the deactivated control wheel MIC/interphone switch. No transmission should be heard on the other pilot’s headset.

Speak into the pilot’s microphone associated with the inoperative switch while simultaneously pushing the associated pilot’s ACP MIC switch. The transmission should be clearly heard on the other pilot’s headset.

MELSubItem.Title: Flight Crew Audio Control Panel MIC/Interphone Switches

ListItem.Para: Affected switch is verified inoperative open.
Associated control wheel MIC/interphone switch operates normally.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Confirm the inoperative audio control panel (ACP) MIC/interphone switch is inoperative open and verify the associated pilot’s control wheel MIC/interphone switch operates normally (AMM DDG 23-51-04).

ListItem.Para: Provide electrical power on the airplane.
Push the FLT transmitter select switch on the captain’s and first officer’s ACPs and ensure the FLT MIC light is illuminated on both panels.
Ensure the FLT receiver light is illuminated and receiver volume control is properly adjusted on both ACPs.

Without pressing MIC on the control wheel, ACP or glareshield, speak into the associated microphone and determine if voice transmission can be heard on the other pilot’s headset. If no voice transmission is heard, the ACP MIC/interphone switch is in the open position.

Speak into the pilot’s microphone associated with the inoperative switch while simultaneously pushing the inoperative ACP MIC/interphone switch. No transmission should be heard on the other pilot’s headset.

Speak into the pilot’s microphone associated with the inoperative switch while simultaneously pushing the associated pilot’s control wheel MIC/interphone switch. The transmission should be clearly heard on the other pilot’s headset.

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative provided microphone switch is deactivated open.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the inoperative glareshield MIC switch and confirm it is deactivated open (AMM DDG 23-51-04).

ListItem.Para: Deactivate the inoperative glareshield MIC switch.
Open the following circuit breakers and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag:
FLT INTERPHONE-CAPT
FLT INTERPHONE-FO
FLT DK LTS-FO INST PNL
FLT DK LTS-STBY INST PNL
Remove the three screws which mount the inoperative switch panel (CLOCK/MAP/MIC) in the glareshield panel.
Pull the inoperative switch panel out of the glareshield panel.
Push out pins 8 and 9 from the electrical connector.
Cap and stow the disconnected wires.
Install the switch panel in the glareshield panel and secure with the three screws.
Close the following circuit breakers:
FLT INTERPHONE-CAPT
FLT INTERPHONE-FO
FLT DK LTS-FO INST PNL
FLT DK LTS-STBY INST PNL
Confirm the inoperative glareshield MIC switch is deactivated open.
Provide electrical power on the airplane.
Push the FLT transmitter select switch on the captain’s and first officer’s ACPs and ensure the FLT MIC light is illuminated on both panels.
Ensure the FLT receiver light is illuminated and receiver volume control is properly adjusted on both ACPs.

Speak into the pilot’s microphone associated with the inoperative switch while simultaneously pushing the deactivated glareshield MIC switch. No transmission should be heard on the other pilot’s headset.
Associated CLOCK/MAP/MIC switch panel is deactivated.
Associated CLOCK function is considered inoperative.
Associated flight compartment illumination system (MAP light) is considered inoperative.

Note.rev:revHighlight: Add additional dispatch item notes (note 1 and note 2) due to provisos (b. and c.) requiring them to be considered inoperative
Note.Para: For associated CLOCK function considered inoperative, the airplane must also be dispatched using MEL item 31-61-08

Note.rev:revHighlight: Add additional dispatch item notes (note 1 and note 2) due to provisos (b. and c.) requiring them to be considered inoperative
Note.Para: For associated flight compartment illumination function (MAP light) considered inoperative, the airplane must also be dispatched using MEL item 33-11-01

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the inoperative CLOCK/MAP/MIC glareshield switch panel (AMM DDG 23-51-04).

ListItem.Para: Deactivate the inoperative glareshield MIC switch.
Open the following circuit breakers and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag:
FLT INTERPHONE-CAPT
FLT INTERPHONE-FO
FLT DK LTS-FO INST PNL
FLT DK LTS-STBY INST PNL
FLT DK LTS-MAP
Remove the three screws which mount the inoperative switch panel (CLOCK/MAP/MIC) in the glareshield panel.
Pull the inoperative switch panel out of the glareshield panel.
Disconnect, cap and stow electrical connector.
Install the switch panel in the glareshield panel and secure with the three screws.
Close the following circuit breakers:
FLT INTERPHONE-CAPT
FLT INTERPHONE-FO
FLT DK LTS-FO INST PNL
FLT DK LTS-STBY INST PNL
FLT DK LTS-MAP
